subject: defconfig: enable macvlan support (#71)
author: Nigel Shearman

upstream.vanilla_quote:
config MACVLAN
	tristate "MAC-VLAN support"
	help
	  This allows one to create virtual interfaces that map packets to
	  or from specific MAC addresses to a particular interface.

provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md: defconfig (11 commits) group, classified as 'config — feeds P1.3'; P1.3 note flags stock-linux.config as 15 months old and lacking CONFIG_MACVLAN=y

impact.effect_if_absent: No MACVLAN virtual network interface support; container networking tools like Docker cannot create multiple virtual MACs on a single interface; network namespace isolation via macvlan is unavailable.

userspace_coupling.interface: socket/ioctl (SIOCADDRT, netlink RTM_* messages via libnl); Docker and container runtimes call ip link add type macvlan or use netlink directly

notes: CONFIG_MACVLAN has been upstream since Linux 2.6.23 (2007, commit b863ceb7ddce [NET]: Add macvlan driver). The fork deliberately enables it in MiSTer_defconfig to support Docker container networking and multi-MAC scenarios. However, CRITICAL CAVEAT: Buildroot (the actual build system) does not use the fork's arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig. Instead, Buildroot is configured (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config) to build 6.18.38 vanilla and uses a separate custom linux.config file (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config in Buildroot) — which DOES NOT include CONFIG_MACVLAN. Therefore, this fork-repository change has NO effect on the actual MiSTer images built by Buildroot. The MiSTer_defconfig appears to be maintained as a reference or for standalone kernel builds from the fork, but not for Buildroot. If MACVLAN support is desired in Buildroot-built images, it must be added to board/mister/de10nano/linux.config, not relying on MiSTer_defconfig. This is a config-level forward-port opportunity (add to linux.config if Docker support is desired) but requires a separate decision outside this commit's scope. Failure mode is silent: if the feature is wanted and not enabled, container networking simply fails without a loud error. Author co-authored with Claude Opus 4.6. | orchestrator-verified (Fable, 2026-07-15): confirmed by direct grep — fork HEAD MiSTer_defconfig:1302 has CONFIG_MACVLAN=y (commit dated 2026-07-08); our stock inventory docs/stock-inventory/stock-linux.config:1302 still has '# CONFIG_MACVLAN is not set' (snapshot PREDATES this stock commit); our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config has NO MACVLAN line (disabled). Disposition corrected carried->not-evaluated: the functionality is NOT in our build and no deliberate drop decision exists. ACTION REQUIRED: config-parity decision — add CONFIG_MACVLAN (=y or =m) to linux.config to match stock-next, or document a deliberate drop. Also a process finding: stock defconfig commits newer than our stock-inventory snapshot are a live drift channel; Phase 2 config reconciliation must diff against fork HEAD defconfig, not only the aged stock-linux.config snapshot. | RESOLVED (user carry decision 2026-07-14/15 (see silent-regressions.md + git d2f2791 follow-ups)): CONFIG_MACVLAN=y added to board/mister/de10nano/linux.config — stock-next parity restored; config drift closed.
